_calibration_scope: native_calibration.jsonl covers tests/differential/{basic,loop_overflow_peel,memory,pyperformance} (808 tests) on CPython 3.14 PLUS a first calibrated slice of tests/differential/stdlib (28 tests, task #55): every silent failure CONFIRMED in a clean isolated jobs=1 re-run (a contended parallel survey produced ~85% build-contention false-fails under heavy multi-agent load, ALL discarded; only mismatch-class fails that reproduced in isolation are seeded). The 28 stdlib seeds = 24 asyncio (taskgroup/ssl/task-factory, #3), 1 array_basic SIGABRT crash, 3 message/API-parity (_bisect, _opcode, argparse subparsers). The REMAINING ~1907 stdlib tests are still UNCALIBRATED (task #55 follow-up): the guard does not reality-check them until a quiet-machine isolated calibration run seeds them (fail-closed against false greenness, loud about the gap). Calibrate with the iso toolchain pattern + tools/calib_daemon.py (jobs=1) documented in the README.
_comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for KNOWN-failing differential tests (task #46). An entry here is a DEBT WITH AN OWNER, not an accepted state; it leaves only by being fixed. Every `fail` dimension carries tracking + root_cause + evidence (machine-checked by tools/check_suite_honesty.py). A passing test is the implicit default and is NOT listed. Tests excluded by design (exec/eval/compile) live in TOO_DYNAMIC_EXPECTED_FAILURE_TESTS; tests with an inline `# MOLT_META: expect_fail=molt` marker are tracked there — neither is duplicated here (the guard forbids overlap).
_compliance_note: The `compliance` block tracks the pytest compliance suite (tests/compliance/py3{12,13,14}/test_spec.py), a DIFFERENT harness from the differential JSONL. The guard LINTS these entries (every `fail`/`uncalibrated` is validated the same way -- tracking + root_cause + evidence on a fail; loud uncalibrated) but does NOT differential-reality-check them (no shared results file). Each key is 'tests/compliance/<pkg>/test_spec.py::<Class>::<method>'. FINDING THIS SESSION: the py314 PEP-649 deferred-annotation case that earlier reports flagged ('2' != '0') now PASSES on native@3.14 (molt defers annotations correctly) -- it is FIXED and deliberately NOT seeded as a debt (mirrors the stale generator-shape entries). The py313 multiline-docstring case is host-version-gated (skips on a 3.14 host) and could not be cleanly re-confirmed here under build contention, so it is marked native@3.13 'uncalibrated' (loud) pending a py3.13 calibration run, rather than asserted or silently dropped.
_dimensions: Keys under `dimensions` are '<backend>' or '<backend>@<cpython-version>'. Backends: native, llvm, wasm, luau. `native` (basic + a 28-test stdlib slice) and `wasm` (4 #59 PEP-695/typing E1-inliner build failures, task #55) are calibrated (against native_calibration.jsonl on CPython 3.14). llvm/wasm/luau are UNCALIBRATED at the suite level pending their own calibration lanes (follow-up task #46-llvm-wasm-luau-calibration); add per-test backend dimensions only with verified evidence from those runs, never from description. Per this session's reviews: generator shapes pre-3fe511c3d are now FIXED (do NOT seed); candidate llvm/wasm debts to verify in that lane include sum int->float #37, alias_reassign bigint-carrier, gen.throw shapes #51 (LLVM dominance + a noted native miscompile).
_root_cause_groups: Many native failures share a root cause; the `tracking` field names the shared bug (e.g. #3 asyncio, #50 generic_class). Per-test entries are kept because the ratchet enforces per-test, but fixing the shared bug should flip a whole group to pass at once — then remove that group's entries.

native@3.13.note: Flagged as a known fail in this session's compliance review (multiline __doc__ stripping). Host is CPython 3.14 (this py313 test skips); a clean molt build to confirm on 3.13 was not obtainable under the concurrent stdlib calibration. UNCALIBRATED pending follow-up #46-stdlib-calibration's py3.13 compliance lane -- do not treat as green.

wasm.evidence: calibrated-run 2026-06-06 wasm@3.14 via tools/wasm_diff.py (node run_wasm.js); deterministic build-fail reproduced 2x isolated on dev (wasm.rs:2196) and release (expected-i64-nothing-on-stack); native build+run byte-identical (task #55 wasm calibration)
wasm.root_cause: E1 inliner back-conversion emits invalid labels compiling the typing module path (typing___typing_strip_wrapping_parens); dev wasm hits the wasm.rs:2196 debug_assert, release wasm fails structural validation with 'expected i64 but nothing on stack'. Native is byte-identical (wasm-only).
